channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Australia’s social media ban for children has already wiped out 4.7 million accounts
item.description: Social media companies have revoked access to about 4.7 million accounts identified as belonging to children in Australia since the country banned use of the platforms by those under 16, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple is facing increasing demands to do more for Iranian protestors
item.description: As protests in Iran intensify, satellite technology has become one of the only ways for people in the country to circumvent a total internet blackout and heavy restrictions on phone service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco finally patches seven-week-old zero-day flaw in Secure Email Gateway products
item.description: Better late than never. Cisco this week patched a ‘critical’ zero-day flaw in the company’s email security and management gateways that has hung over customers’ heads since December.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump pushes for emergency power auction to fuel AI boom
item.description: The Trump administration laid out a plan intended to compel technology companies to effectively fund the construction of new power plants as a way to tame surging consumer utility bills while aiding the development of data centers.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Cisco has finally patched a maximum-level security issue which was allegedly being targeted by Chinese hackers
item.description: A maximum-severity Cisco bug was being exploited by multiple Chinese actors, reports said, forcing the firm to act.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI, Microsoft lose last chance to avoid trial with Elon Musk
item.description: A federal judge in Oakland rejected requests by OpenAI and Microsoft to dismiss claims by Musk and ordered the case to proceed to a jury trial set for late April.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: NordVPN just made it easier to browse from 30 'hard-to-reach' countries
item.description: NordVPN has expanded its server network with nearly 30 new virtual locations, granting users IP addresses in regions where physical infrastructure is scarce or insecure. Crucially, the provider is staying transparent about where those servers are actually located.
item.dc:creator: Rene Millman, TechRadar

item.title: MongoDB releases mongot source code to boost RAG and AI workloads
item.description: MongoDB has released the source code of mongot, the engine that powers MongoDB Search and Vector Search, under the Server Side Public License.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to watch Dirty Talk: When Daytime Talk Shows Ruled TV online from anywhere
item.description: Want to watch Dirty Talk: When Daytime Talk Shows Ruled TV from anywhere? Stream every wild reveal as daytime TV’s most chaotic era gets exposed.
item.dc:creator: Krishi Chowdhary, TechRadar

item.title: How Ansible does the real work in hyperautomation
item.description: Forget the sci-fi fantasy of robots running everything. The real story isn’t about flashy tech, it’s about a smarter way to work. Hyperautomation is that smarter way: A deliberate, all-hands-on-deck strategy where organizations systematically find, prioritize and overhaul their processes.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Gleam update shines on external types
item.description: Gleam 1.14.0, a new version of the statically typed language for the Erlang VM and JavaScript runtimes, has enhanced support for external types. Released December 25, the update can be accessed at GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This old Pennsylvania coal town could get a reboot from AI
item.description: As the September evening inched along, the line of residents waiting their turn for the microphone held steady. Filing down the auditorium aisles at the Indiana University of Pennsylvania, they were armed with questions about a new gas plant slated for their community.
item.dc:creator: Capital & Main, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s Grok AI faces probe by Canada over sexualized deepfakes
item.description: Canada's Privacy Commissioner joined the list of regulators expanding its investigations into Elon Musk's artificial intelligence service, Grok, after the app was used to create non-consensual, explicit images of people.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: EY exec: If you think agentic AI is a challenge, you’re not ready for what’s coming
item.description: Companies struggling to keep up with the arrival of AI agents should buckle up: even more complicated technologies are quickly coming down the pike. That includes physical AI, which includes robots — and which Nvidia pegs as a multibillion-dollar market —and quantum computing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: For agentic AI, other disciplines need their own Git
item.description: Software engineering didn’t adopt AI agents faster because engineers are more adventurous, or the use case was better. They adopted them more quickly because they already had Git.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: React tutorial: Get started with the React JavaScript library
item.description: Despite many worthy contenders, React remains the most popular front-end framework, and a key player in the JavaScript development landscape. React is the quintessential reactive engine, continually innovating alongside the rest of the industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World
